batches.recipe_prompt: Mountain biker descending a rocky forest trail, wearing full-face downhill helmet, protective gloves, and jersey with armor, the wearable remote from image 1 with its velcro strap wrapped fully around the LEFT lower forearm OVER the jersey sleeve, positioned halfway between wrist and elbow, PLUS button closest to wrist, five round tactile buttons, both hands on handlebars, waist-up, Shot on Sony A7R IV 85mm f/2.0, dappled forest light, film grain

Female motorcycle rider threading through traffic on a suspension bridge at golden hour, downtown skyline rising behind her catching warm amber light. Waist-up framing captured on Sony A7R IV with 85mm f/2.0 lens, shallow depth of field isolating the rider against softly blurred traffic and glowing city towers. Rim light wraps around her silhouette from the low sun, creating a golden halo effect. Bridge cables slice through the frame diagonally, leading the eye toward the backlit urban skyline. Warm streetlight glow beginning to compete with natural sunset tones. Film grain texture, photorealistic rendering with dynamic motion blur on passing vehicles, asphalt reflecting streaks of orange and gold.. female motorcycle rider wearing full-face helmet, leather riding jacket with armor, thick leather riding gloves, the wearable remote from image 1 with its velcro strap wrapped fully around the LEFT lower forearm OVER the sleeve, positioned halfway between wrist and elbow, PLUS (+) button closest to WRIST, MINUS (−) button closest to elbow, LED window closest to wrist, five round tactile buttons in a vertical row, device parallel to the forearm not perpendicular, waist-up, warm golden-hour light, film grain.

Mountain biker descending a rocky forest trail, wearing full-face downhill helmet, protective gloves, and long-sleeve jersey with armor, the wearable remote from image 1 with its velcro strap wrapped fully around the LEFT lower forearm OVER the sleeve, positioned halfway between wrist and elbow, PLUS (+) button closest to WRIST, MINUS (−) button closest to elbow, LED window closest to wrist, five round tactile buttons in a vertical row, device parallel to the forearm not perpendicular, both hands on handlebars, waist-up, Shot on Sony A7R IV 85mm f/2.0, dappled forest light, film grain

A mountain biker tears through dense forest singletrack, glancing back over their shoulder as decaying zombies lurch through the underbrush in pursuit. Overhead sun slices through the canopy creating stark pools of light and deep shadow pockets across the trail. The rider navigates exposed roots and tight tree corridors while supernatural pursuers emerge from the darker recesses of the woods. Harsh midday light creates extreme contrast between sunlit trail sections and shadowed forest depths, with dust particles and displaced leaves hanging in the bright rays. The scene captures the mountain biker mid-flight over a log obstacle, zombies reaching from both sides of the trail, their forms partially silhouetted against bright background clearings. Gnarled branches create a claustrophobic frame while the relentless overhead sun bleaches exposed trail patches to near-white, leaving rotting pursuers half-visible in the contrasting shadows. The composition balances the clean technical lines of the bike and rider against the chaotic, deteriorating forms of the undead, all unified by the unforgiving midday lighting that refuses to hide any detail of either chase participant or the challenging terrain between them.. mountain biker wearing full-face DH helmet, gloves, long-sleeve jersey with armor, the compact wearable remote from image 1 (108mm long, 39mm wide) with its velcro strap wrapped fully around the LEFT lower forearm OVER the jacket sleeve, positioned halfway between wrist and elbow, LED window closest to WRIST, PLUS button closest to wrist, MINUS button closest to elbow, five round tactile buttons in a vertical row reading from wrist to elbow: PLUS (+), FAST-FORWARD (two right triangles), PLAY/PAUSE (three bars with triangle), REWIND (two left triangles), MINUS (−), button symbols matching the product in image 2, device parallel to the forearm not perpendicular, waist-up, harsh midday sun, sharp shadows.

A female rider carves through fog-draped asphalt lined with gnarled, skeletal trees under overcast twilight skies. Shot with a 35mm lens at f/2.8, natural low light captures the eerie atmosphere—mist swirling in her wake, dead leaves scattering across cracked pavement. Panning motion blur at 1/250s follows her through the haunting corridor.
